source_units.text_raw:
This chapter aims to manage parking supply in a way that supports urban vitality, climate goals, housing affordability, and equitable mobility. Parking is treated as shared infrastructure to be managed, not maximized. Maximizing parking imposes significant economic, environmental, and social costs on cities. Land consumed by surface parking lots represents a major opportunity cost, displacing housing, businesses, and public spaces that could contribute more meaningfully to urban vitality and tax revenues. Reducing parking minimums can therefore unlock more equitable, compact, and climate- conscious development and spur improved transit in Charlottetown.
Unless otherwise specified, off-street parking shall be provided in accordance with the parking requirements in Tables 8.1, 8.2, and 8.3.
There are no minimum parking requirements in the 500 Lot area of Charlottetown which is inclusive of the DC, DMS, DMU, DN and DW zones.
The Development Officer may accept less parking than is required in this bylaw on a site if a traffic impact study (TIS) can demonstrate a rationale for less parking.
New additions, which result in an increase in the gross floor area greater than 25%, shall require additional parking over and above the existing parking spaces following Section 8.2.1.
Renovations and alterations, which result in an increase in the gross floor area less than 25%, shall not require any additional parking, but the number of spaces which existed prior to the renovations, alterations, shall not be reduced.
Where a building is repaired to the same use after a fire or other catastrophe, the original number of parking spaces is adequate even if it does not meet the current parking requirements.
Where a parking calculation for a development in any zone results in a fraction of a parking space, the final number of required parking spaces shall be rounded down to the next whole number to a minimum of 1 space.
Parking must be located on the lot being developed addressing any built-form requirements detailed in the zone.
Where a building or lot use is used for more than one use,:
Cash-in-lieu of parking spaces may be accepted outside of the 500 Lot Area in any situation where adequate parking cannot be provided or is unfeasible on a lot.
All funds obtained through the cash-in-lieu provisions shall be used exclusively for the provision of additional parking spaces or improved mobility facilities (bike lanes, transit shelters, bike parking, etc) in the downtown area.
All parking spaces, except stacked spaces, shall have unobstructed access to a public street by means of a driveway, aisle, or lane.
'Stacked' parking spaces can be counted as a parking space for the RN, RM, RH, GC and GN Zones.
An automobile parking space shall have minimum dimension of not less than 2.6 m (8.5 ft) by 5.5 m (18.0 ft).
Parking spaces for trailer trucks, buses, delivery trucks, motor homes or other large vehicles shall have dimensions no less than 3.05 m (10 ft) by 12.2 m (40 ft) or larger, if necessary to accommodate longer vehicles.
Parking aisles shall be no less than 6.1m (20 ft) wide for right angle parking, 5.49 m (18.0 ft) for 60 degree parking, 3.35 m (11.0 ft) for 45 degree or less Type of Use Minimum Parking Requirement Clubs, Educational Facilities, Places of Entertainment, Places of Worship, Theatres, Churches, Auditorium, Academic Research and Administration Buildings, Theatres, Arenas, Halls, Stadium, and other places of assembly. 1 parking space per 30 m 2 of gross floor area. Eating and Drinking Establishment, Cocktail lounge, tavern or Club, restaurant or drive- through 1 space per 30 m 2 of gross floor area; plus queuing space for drive-through as required Day-Care Facilities 1 space for each working employee plus a drop-off area or 2 parking spaces for the parents Laundromat 1 space for every 10 Washing machines Animal Shelter 1 space for every 28 m 2 of floor space. Funeral Establishment A minimum of 15 spaces plus 1 space for each 5 seats Office, Retail Store, Retail Service Shop 1 space per 25 m 2 of gross floor area; 0 spaces required for the 500 Lot area Convenience Store 1 space per 25 m 2 of gross floor area; 0 spaces required for the 500 Lot area Shopping Centre and Retail Store 1 space per 25 m 2 gross floor area Tourist Accommodations 1 space per 3 bedrooms or guest rooms, plus 1 space for the operator/proprietor (additional space not required for lodging houses) Hotel 1 space for every 25 m 2 of commercial or Office space in the hotel; plus 1 space per room. 1 tour bus space may be substituted for vehicle parking for every 18 rooms or suites up to a maximum of 36 rooms or suites. Industrial or warehousing Establishment Manufacturing 1 space for each 23 m 2 of Office space plus 1 space for each additional 30 m 2 of Floor Area of Manufacturing area plus 1 space for each 93 m2 of warehousing area (exclusive of Loading Spaces, truck parking, and Landscaped Areas) or 1 space for each working employee, whichever is greater Marina 1 space per every 3 boat berths and mooring areas Storage Facility 1 space for each 23 m 2 of Office space plus 1 space per 300 m 2 of Gross Floor Area dedicated to storage or 1 parking space per twenty (20) storage units, whichever is lesser Marijuana Production Facility 1 space per 200 m 2 of Floor area or 1 space per working employee, whichever is greater. Any use not specified above. 1 parking space per 30 m 2 of gross floor area. Table 8.2 Commercial, Office & Industrial Parking Requirements Table 8.3 Institutional and Public Use Parking Requirements Type of Use Minimum Parking Requirement Hospitals and nursing homes 1 parking space for each 4 beds or each 30 m 2 of gross floor area, whichever is less. School, Elementary 2 spaces for every classroom School, Junior High 3 spaces for every classroom School, Senior High 5 spaces for every classroom University or College 1 space per 200 m 2 of gross floor area Dormitory 1 space for every 3 beds or 0.5 space per unit, whichever is less Medical, Health, and Dental Clinics 2 spaces per 1,000 ft 2 . Community Care Facility 1 space per 3 bedrooms, or 1 space per 4 bedrooms in the Lot 500 Area Cultural Establishment 1 space per 50 m 2 of gross floor area plus 1 space per working employee Library 1 space per 100 m 2 of gross floor area Active Recreation facilities e.g. baseball, soccer, tennis, pickleball 1 space per court, 20 per soccer/baseball field Parks No requirement Any use not specified above 1 parking space per 30 m 2 of gross floor area.
Where four (4) or more parking spaces are required for the land use on a lot, the following minimum requirements shall apply:
The parking lot shall be on a hard surface (asphalt, pavers, concrete) to prevent the raising of dust and to prevent tracking materials on to a street;
Parking lots shall address the stormwater management requirements of this bylaw draining to a central public storm system or to an on-site retention system or combination of both. At no time shall water from a parking lot drain onto adjoining lots
The City may require a stormwater plan and grading plan stamped by a civil engineer or landscape architect in accordance with the City of Charlottetown Stormwater Management Guidelines.
Any light used for illumination of the parking lot or parking station shall be arranged so as to direct light away from adjacent lots;
All pedestrian access to the main door(s) of the building from the parking lot shall be designed with minimum 1.2 m (4 ft) width pedestrian pathway that are hard surfaced with asphalt, concrete, or unit pavers;
A land use buffer, satisfactory to the Development Officer, consisting of a 1 m wide landscape treatment, a fence and/or mature trees (spaced no more than 6.1 m apart), or both, shall be provided where a parking lot abuts a residential zone or a building occupied for residential use in a commercial zone;
The access to the parking lot shall conform with the Street Access Bylaw requirements;
A structure of not more than 3.66 m (12 ft) in height and 4.65 m 2 (50.1 ft 2 ) in area may be erected on a parking lot for the use of attendants, and it shall be painted and maintained in good condition;
The City may, where for safety reasons due to traffic volumes and the number of existing access points to an arterial or collector Street, require adjoining property owners to share the access to their lots and parking, or the City may refuse a new access to a lot.
For parking lots exceeding 30 stalls, the lot shall include landscape islands:
At a rate of one island per 15 parking spaces over the initial 30 stalls and one island per 30 stalls for the remaining parking spaces;
That have a minimum width of 2.5 m for the full length of the stall and contain at least one deciduous tree and a combination of shrubs, ornamental grasses and/or ground covers;
That includes curbs and gutters;
That may be designed as stormwater gardens for stormwater management;
That may include a 1 m wide concrete walkway if the island width is increased to 3 m.
STANDARDS
Off-street accessible parking spaces shall measure at least 3.6 m wide and shall contain:
Paint markings that include the accessible logo stencil and access aisle paint marking;
Accessible parking sign at the back of the sidewalk;
An accessible curb cut or mountable curb where curbs are present
Where the limits of the parking lot are defined by a curb, the parking lot shall be provided with a ramped curb as close as possible to the location to an accessible entrance or elevators and in no case, shall it be further than 50 m (164 ft) from the location which it is intended to serve, with the exception of a university or college which has a central campus and perimeter parking already established.
The accessible parking space(s) shall be situated as close as possible to the entrance location it is intended to serve.
Each reserved parking space Shall be clearly identified by a ground sign.
Accessible parking spaces shall be provided at a rate as shown in Table 8.4 and count towards the overall parking requirement.
For all single detached homes, no more than 35% of the area of the front or flankage yard shall be dedicated to driveways or parking;
For all semi detached and duplex homes, no more than 50% of the area of the front or Figure 8.1 Parking Island Requirements /uni0050/uni0061/uni0072/uni006B/uni0069/uni006E/uni0067/uni0020/uni0049/uni0073/uni006C/uni0061/uni006E/uni0064 /uni004C/uni0061/uni006E/uni0064/uni0073/uni0063/uni0061/uni0070/uni0069/uni006E/uni0067/uni0020/uni006F/uni0072/uni0020/uni0053/uni0074/uni006F/uni0072/uni006D/uni0077/uni0061/uni0074/uni0065/uni0072 /uni004D/uni0069/uni006E/uni002E/uni0020/uni0050/uni0061/uni0072/uni006B/uni0069/uni006E/uni0067/uni0020/uni0053/uni0074/uni0061/uni006C/uni006C/uni0020 /uni0028/uni0032/uni002E/uni0036/uni006D/uni0020/uni0078/uni0020/uni0035/uni002E/uni0035/uni006D/uni0029 /uni0031/uni0035/uni0030/uni006D/uni006D/uni0020/uni0050/uni0061/uni0069/uni006E/uni0074 /uni0043/uni0075/uni0072/uni0062/uni0020/uni0061/uni006E/uni0064/uni0020/uni0047/uni0075/uni0074/uni0074/uni0065/uni0072 /uni0031/uni006D/uni0020/uni0052/uni0061/uni0064/uni0069/uni0075/uni0073 /uni0054/uni0072/uni0065/uni0065/uni0073/uni0020/uni0028/uni006D/uni0069/uni006E/uni002E/uni0020/uni0036/uni0030/uni006D/uni006D/uni0020/uni0043/uni0061/uni006C/uni0069/uni0070/uni0065/uni0072/uni0029 /uni0020/uni0032/uni002E/uni0035/uni0020/uni006D/uni0020 flankage yard shall be dedicated to driveways or parking;
For all townhouses, no more than 80% of the area of the front or flankage yard shall be dedicated to driveways or parking;
Driveway widths for any residential lots shall meet the minimum and maximum dimensions as outlined in the Street Access Bylaw for any given use.
Parking lots for multi-unit dwellings are not permitted between the street and the building except where the building fronts two (2) or more streets.
Porte cochere's or driveway drop-offs for multi-unit dwellings entries are permitted between the building and the street with 2 temporary parking stalls.
The Development Officer may approve shared parking facilities, subject to the following standards;
Shared parking arrangements are permitted between two or more uses that demonstrate offsetting peak parking demand (e.g., daytime offices with evening residential uses).
Owners must provide a signed legal agreement specifying the shared parking arrangement, duration, maintenance responsibilities, and enforcement measures. Such agreements must be approved by the
Queueing spaces shall be provided in accordance with the following requirements:
Businesses must ensure all vehicles waiting for service remain within their property boundaries. Queueing vehicles on public roadways is prohibited.
If queueing issues persist on public streets, the Development Officer may impose additional operational restrictions or modifications, including limiting hours of operation, fines or mandating additional on-site queueing capacity or traffic management measures.
Queueing lanes must be clearly marked, separated from parking areas and pedestrian walkways, and designed to Level 1 Electric Vehicle Charging: An electric vehicle charging method that uses a standard 120-volt AC power outlet, providing a slow charging rate typically sufficient for overnight charging or extended parking periods. Level 1 chargers supply power at approximately 2-5 kilometres of driving range per hour of charging. Level 2 Electric Vehicle (EV) Charging: A charging station that provides electric vehicle charging at 208-240 volts AC, capable of delivering power typically ranging from 3.3 to 19.2 kW, allowing vehicles to recharge fully within several hours, suitable for commercial, institutional, and residential use.
Within the 500 Lot Area, dedicated parking garages shall:
Provide groundfloor retail uses for no less than 50% of any street frontage to a minimum depth of no less than 6 m;
Require screening of parking levels above ground floor with architectural elements such as louvres, perforated metal, glazing, or green walls to obscure direct views of parked vehicles;
Be made of high-quality, durable, and visually appealing materials sympathetic to the surrounding architecture for all visible facades;
Facade design should complement the surrounding built form, respecting height, materials, and rhythm of adjacent buildings.
Where feasible, incorporate weather- protected sidewalks, bike parking, or small seating areas along the frontage.
Include landscaping or other softening treatments along sidewalks and entrances to enhance pedestrian environment and mitigate visual impact.
Parking garage entries on frontages shall not exceed 7 m wide and more than 30% of the buildings frontage.
The minimum required bicycle parking spaces shall be provided in accordance with the bicycle parking requirements in Table 8.5. Decimal results shall be rounded up.
Notwithstanding the ratios required for class A and B bike parking, class A bike parking can be provided at a higher than the recommended percentage to meet the bike parking requirements up to 100%.
Bicycle parking racks shall be required to be spaced
At least 0.45 m apart for a vertical rack or two-tier rack with a lift assist, or
At least 0.9 m apart for other types of rack. 8.13 BICYCLE PARKING (CLASS A)
Class A bicycle parking shall be located:
Within a room that is dedicated to the storage of bicycles;
Within a roofed bicycle cage outside of a building;
Within an enclosed bicycle locker outside of a building;
Within a covered parking garage area reserved for bicycles; or
Within a resident storage unit located in an indoor parking area that is associated with a multi-unit dwelling use.
On the ground floor of the building; or
Within one storey of a ground floor and be:
Accessible from a ground floor with ramps, which are protected from motor vehicle traffic, or
Accessible from a ground floor by elevator.
Any Class A bicycle storage shall be secured against unauthorized entry.
Parking stalls shall be no less than 0.6 m (2 ft) long and 1.2 m (3.9 ft) high, and have an associated aisle of 1.5 m in width. 8.14 BICYCLE PARKING (CLASS B)
Class B bicycle parking shall:
Be located outside of a building in a location that is visible and accessible from the street, within 20 m of a main door;
Be surfaced with a hard material such as asphalt, concrete, or unit pavers;
Include galvanized, powder coated or stainless steel racks spaced 0.90 m width apart with a clear bike parking length of 1.8 m. Table 8.5 Required Bicycle Parking Spaces by Use Use Bike Parking Requirement Ratio of Class A and B Multi-unit Dwelling 1 space per 4 units 80% class A, 20% class B Hotels 1 space/ 30 rooms 50% class A, 50% class B Commercial/Office 1 space/ 500 m 2 GFA 50% class A, 50% class B Entertainment Establishment 1 space/ 25 seats 20% class A, 80% class B Institutional uses 1 space/ 250 m 2 GFA 100% class B Parking Structure 1 space/ 20 parking stalls 100% class B Any other uses 1 space/ 500 m 2 GFA 50% class A, 50% class B /uni0020/uni0031/uni002E/uni0035/uni0020/uni006D/uni0020 /uni0020/uni0031/uni002E/uni0032/uni0020/uni006D/uni0020 /uni0020/uni0030/uni002E/uni0039/uni0020/uni006D/uni0020 /uni0042/uni0069/uni006B/uni0065/uni0020/uni0041/uni0069/uni0073/uni006C/uni0065 /uni0042/uni0069/uni006B/uni0065/uni0020/uni0052/uni0061/uni0063/uni006B Figure 8.3 Class B Bike Parking

clauses_raw.clause_text_raw: This chapter aims to manage parking supply in a way that supports urban vitality, climate goals, housing affordability, and equitable mobility. Parking is treated as shared infrastructure to be managed, not maximized. Maximizing parking imposes significant economic, environmental, and social costs on cities. Land consumed by surface parking lots represents a major opportunity cost, displacing housing, businesses, and public spaces that could contribute more meaningfully to urban vitality and tax revenues. Reducing parking minimums can therefore unlock more equitable, compact, and climate- conscious development and spur improved transit in Charlottetown.
Unless otherwise specified, off-street parking shall be provided in accordance with the parking requirements in Tables 8.1, 8.2, and 8.3.
There are no minimum parking requirements in the 500 Lot area of Charlottetown which is inclusive of the DC, DMS, DMU, DN and DW zones.
The Development Officer may accept less parking than is required in this bylaw on a site if a traffic impact study (TIS) can demonstrate a rationale for less parking.
New additions, which result in an increase in the gross floor area greater than 25%, shall require additional parking over and above the existing parking spaces following Section 8.2.1.

Renovations and alterations, which result in an increase in the gross floor area less than 25%, shall not require any additional parking, but the number of spaces which existed prior to the renovations, alterations, shall not be reduced.
Where a building is repaired to the same use after a fire or other catastrophe, the original number of parking spaces is adequate even if it does not meet the current parking requirements.

Where a parking calculation for a development in any zone results in a fraction of a parking space, the final number of required parking spaces shall be rounded down to the next whole number to a minimum of 1 space.
Parking must be located on the lot being developed addressing any built-form requirements detailed in the zone.
Where a building or lot use is used for more than one use,:
Cash-in-lieu of parking spaces may be accepted outside of the 500 Lot Area in any situation where adequate parking cannot be provided or is unfeasible on a lot.

All funds obtained through the cash-in-lieu provisions shall be used exclusively for the provision of additional parking spaces or improved mobility facilities (bike lanes, transit shelters, bike parking, etc) in the downtown area.
All parking spaces, except stacked spaces, shall have unobstructed access to a public street by means of a driveway, aisle, or lane.
'Stacked' parking spaces can be counted as a parking space for the RN, RM, RH, GC and GN Zones.
An automobile parking space shall have minimum dimension of not less than 2.6 m (8.5 ft) by 5.5 m (18.0 ft).

Parking spaces for trailer trucks, buses, delivery trucks, motor homes or other large vehicles shall have dimensions no less than 3.05 m (10 ft) by 12.2 m (40 ft) or larger, if necessary to accommodate longer vehicles.

Parking aisles shall be no less than 6.1m (20 ft) wide for right angle parking, 5.49 m (18.0 ft) for 60 degree parking, 3.35 m (11.0 ft) for 45 degree or less Type of Use Minimum Parking Requirement Clubs, Educational Facilities, Places of Entertainment, Places of Worship, Theatres, Churches, Auditorium, Academic Research and Administration Buildings, Theatres, Arenas, Halls, Stadium, and other places of assembly. 1 parking space per 30 m 2 of gross floor area. Eating and Drinking Establishment, Cocktail lounge, tavern or Club, restaurant or drive- through 1 space per 30 m 2 of gross floor area; plus queuing space for drive-through as required Day-Care Facilities 1 space for each working employee plus a drop-off area or 2 parking spaces for the parents Laundromat 1 space for every 10 Washing machines Animal Shelter 1 space for every 28 m 2 of floor space. Funeral Establishment A minimum of 15 spaces plus 1 space for each 5 seats Office, Retail Store, Retail Service Shop 1 space per 25 m 2 of gross floor area; 0 spaces required for the 500 Lot area Convenience Store 1 space per 25 m 2 of gross floor area; 0 spaces required for the 500 Lot area Shopping Centre and Retail Store 1 space per 25 m 2 gross floor area Tourist Accommodations 1 space per 3 bedrooms or guest rooms, plus 1 space for the operator/proprietor (additional space not required for lodging houses) Hotel 1 space for every 25 m 2 of commercial or Office space in the hotel; plus 1 space per room. 1 tour bus space may be substituted for vehicle parking for every 18 rooms or suites up to a maximum of 36 rooms or suites. Industrial or warehousing Establishment Manufacturing 1 space for each 23 m 2 of Office space plus 1 space for each additional 30 m 2 of Floor Area of Manufacturing area plus 1 space for each 93 m2 of warehousing area (exclusive of Loading Spaces, truck parking, and Landscaped Areas) or 1 space for each working employee, whichever is greater Marina 1 space per every 3 boat berths and mooring areas Storage Facility 1 space for each 23 m 2 of Office space plus 1 space per 300 m 2 of Gross Floor Area dedicated to storage or 1 parking space per twenty (20) storage units, whichever is lesser Marijuana Production Facility 1 space per 200 m 2 of Floor area or 1 space per working employee, whichever is greater. Any use not specified above. 1 parking space per 30 m 2 of gross floor area. Table 8.2 Commercial, Office & Industrial Parking Requirements Table 8.3 Institutional and Public Use Parking Requirements Type of Use Minimum Parking Requirement Hospitals and nursing homes 1 parking space for each 4 beds or each 30 m 2 of gross floor area, whichever is less. School, Elementary 2 spaces for every classroom School, Junior High 3 spaces for every classroom School, Senior High 5 spaces for every classroom University or College 1 space per 200 m 2 of gross floor area Dormitory 1 space for every 3 beds or 0.5 space per unit, whichever is less Medical, Health, and Dental Clinics 2 spaces per 1,000 ft 2 . Community Care Facility 1 space per 3 bedrooms, or 1 space per 4 bedrooms in the Lot 500 Area Cultural Establishment 1 space per 50 m 2 of gross floor area plus 1 space per working employee Library 1 space per 100 m 2 of gross floor area Active Recreation facilities e.g. baseball, soccer, tennis, pickleball 1 space per court, 20 per soccer/baseball field Parks No requirement Any use not specified above 1 parking space per 30 m 2 of gross floor area.
Where four (4) or more parking spaces are required for the land use on a lot, the following minimum requirements shall apply:
The parking lot shall be on a hard surface (asphalt, pavers, concrete) to prevent the raising of dust and to prevent tracking materials on to a street;

Parking lots shall address the stormwater management requirements of this bylaw draining to a central public storm system or to an on-site retention system or combination of both. At no time shall water from a parking lot drain onto adjoining lots
The City may require a stormwater plan and grading plan stamped by a civil engineer or landscape architect in accordance with the City of Charlottetown Stormwater Management Guidelines.
Any light used for illumination of the parking lot or parking station shall be arranged so as to direct light away from adjacent lots;

All pedestrian access to the main door(s) of the building from the parking lot shall be designed with minimum 1.2 m (4 ft) width pedestrian pathway that are hard surfaced with asphalt, concrete, or unit pavers;

A land use buffer, satisfactory to the Development Officer, consisting of a 1 m wide landscape treatment, a fence and/or mature trees (spaced no more than 6.1 m apart), or both, shall be provided where a parking lot abuts a residential zone or a building occupied for residential use in a commercial zone;
The access to the parking lot shall conform with the Street Access Bylaw requirements;

A structure of not more than 3.66 m (12 ft) in height and 4.65 m 2 (50.1 ft 2 ) in area may be erected on a parking lot for the use of attendants, and it shall be painted and maintained in good condition;

The City may, where for safety reasons due to traffic volumes and the number of existing access points to an arterial or collector Street, require adjoining property owners to share the access to their lots and parking, or the City may refuse a new access to a lot.
For parking lots exceeding 30 stalls, the lot shall include landscape islands:
At a rate of one island per 15 parking spaces over the initial 30 stalls and one island per 30 stalls for the remaining parking spaces;
That have a minimum width of 2.5 m for the full length of the stall and contain at least one deciduous tree and a combination of shrubs, ornamental grasses and/or ground covers;
That includes curbs and gutters;
That may be designed as stormwater gardens for stormwater management;
That may include a 1 m wide concrete walkway if the island width is increased to 3 m.
STANDARDS
Off-street accessible parking spaces shall measure at least 3.6 m wide and shall contain:
Paint markings that include the accessible logo stencil and access aisle paint marking;
Accessible parking sign at the back of the sidewalk;
An accessible curb cut or mountable curb where curbs are present

Where the limits of the parking lot are defined by a curb, the parking lot shall be provided with a ramped curb as close as possible to the location to an accessible entrance or elevators and in no case, shall it be further than 50 m (164 ft) from the location which it is intended to serve, with the exception of a university or college which has a central campus and perimeter parking already established.
The accessible parking space(s) shall be situated as close as possible to the entrance location it is intended to serve.
Each reserved parking space Shall be clearly identified by a ground sign.
Accessible parking spaces shall be provided at a rate as shown in Table 8.4 and count towards the overall parking requirement.

Level 1 chargers supply power at approximately 2-5 kilometres of driving range per hour of charging. Level 2 Electric Vehicle (EV) Charging: A charging station that provides electric vehicle charging at 208-240 volts AC, capable of delivering power typically ranging from 3.3 to 19.2 kW, allowing vehicles to recharge fully within several hours, suitable for commercial, institutional, and residential use.

Include landscaping or other softening treatments along sidewalks and entrances to enhance pedestrian environment and mitigate visual impact.
Parking garage entries on frontages shall not exceed 7 m wide and more than 30% of the buildings frontage.

At least 0.45 m apart for a vertical rack or two-tier rack with a lift assist, or
At least 0.9 m apart for other types of rack. 8.13 BICYCLE PARKING (CLASS A)

Within a room that is dedicated to the storage of bicycles;

Accessible from a ground floor by elevator.

Be located outside of a building in a location that is visible and accessible from the street, within 20 m of a main door;
Be surfaced with a hard material such as asphalt, concrete, or unit pavers;

Include galvanized, powder coated or stainless steel racks spaced 0.90 m width apart with a clear bike parking length of 1.8 m. Table 8.5 Required Bicycle Parking Spaces by Use Use Bike Parking Requirement Ratio of Class A and B Multi-unit Dwelling 1 space per 4 units 80% class A, 20% class B Hotels 1 space/ 30 rooms 50% class A, 50% class B Commercial/Office 1 space/ 500 m 2 GFA 50% class A, 50% class B Entertainment Establishment 1 space/ 25 seats 20% class A, 80% class B Institutional uses 1 space/ 250 m 2 GFA 100% class B Parking Structure 1 space/ 20 parking stalls 100% class B Any other uses 1 space/ 500 m 2 GFA 50% class A, 50% class B /uni0020/uni0031/uni002E/uni0035/uni0020/uni006D/uni0020 /uni0020/uni0031/uni002E/uni0032/uni0020/uni006D/uni0020 /uni0020/uni0030/uni002E/uni0039/uni0020/uni006D/uni0020 /uni0042/uni0069/uni006B/uni0065/uni0020/uni0041/uni0069/uni0073/uni006C/uni0065 /uni0042/uni0069/uni006B/uni0065/uni0020/uni0052/uni0061/uni0063/uni006B Figure 8.3 Class B Bike Parking

numeric_values.value_raw: 200 m 2 of gross floor area Dormitory 1 space for every 3 beds or 0.5 space per unit, whichever is less Medical, Health, and Dental Clinics 2 spaces per 1,000 ft

requirements.requirement_text_raw: An automobile parking space shall have minimum dimension of not less than 2.6 m (8.5 ft) by 5.5 m (18.0 ft).

Parking aisles shall be no less than 6.1m (20 ft) wide for right angle parking, 5.49 m (18.0 ft) for 60 degree parking, 3.35 m (11.0 ft) for 45 degree or less Type of Use Minimum Parking Requirement Clubs, Educational Facilities, Places of Entertainment, Places of Worship, Theatres, Churches, Auditorium, Academic Research and Administration Buildings, Theatres, Arenas, Halls, Stadium, and other places of assembly. 1 parking space per 30 m 2 of gross floor area. Eating and Drinking Establishment, Cocktail lounge, tavern or Club, restaurant or drive- through 1 space per 30 m 2 of gross floor area; plus queuing space for drive-through as required Day-Care Facilities 1 space for each working employee plus a drop-off area or 2 parking spaces for the parents Laundromat 1 space for every 10 Washing machines Animal Shelter 1 space for every 28 m 2 of floor space. Funeral Establishment A minimum of 15 spaces plus 1 space for each 5 seats Office, Retail Store, Retail Service Shop 1 space per 25 m 2 of gross floor area; 0 spaces required for the 500 Lot area Convenience Store 1 space per 25 m 2 of gross floor area; 0 spaces required for the 500 Lot area Shopping Centre and Retail Store 1 space per 25 m 2 gross floor area Tourist Accommodations 1 space per 3 bedrooms or guest rooms, plus 1 space for the operator/proprietor (additional space not required for lodging houses) Hotel 1 space for every 25 m 2 of commercial or Office space in the hotel; plus 1 space per room. 1 tour bus space may be substituted for vehicle parking for every 18 rooms or suites up to a maximum of 36 rooms or suites. Industrial or warehousing Establishment Manufacturing 1 space for each 23 m 2 of Office space plus 1 space for each additional 30 m 2 of Floor Area of Manufacturing area plus 1 space for each 93 m2 of warehousing area (exclusive of Loading Spaces, truck parking, and Landscaped Areas) or 1 space for each working employee, whichever is greater Marina 1 space per every 3 boat berths and mooring areas Storage Facility 1 space for each 23 m 2 of Office space plus 1 space per 300 m 2 of Gross Floor Area dedicated to storage or 1 parking space per twenty (20) storage units, whichever is lesser Marijuana Production Facility 1 space per 200 m 2 of Floor area or 1 space per working employee, whichever is greater. Any use not specified above. 1 parking space per 30 m 2 of gross floor area. Table 8.2 Commercial, Office & Industrial Parking Requirements Table 8.3 Institutional and Public Use Parking Requirements Type of Use Minimum Parking Requirement Hospitals and nursing homes 1 parking space for each 4 beds or each 30 m 2 of gross floor area, whichever is less. School, Elementary 2 spaces for every classroom School, Junior High 3 spaces for every classroom School, Senior High 5 spaces for every classroom University or College 1 space per 200 m 2 of gross floor area Dormitory 1 space for every 3 beds or 0.5 space per unit, whichever is less Medical, Health, and Dental Clinics 2 spaces per 1,000 ft 2 . Community Care Facility 1 space per 3 bedrooms, or 1 space per 4 bedrooms in the Lot 500 Area Cultural Establishment 1 space per 50 m 2 of gross floor area plus 1 space per working employee Library 1 space per 100 m 2 of gross floor area Active Recreation facilities e.g. baseball, soccer, tennis, pickleball 1 space per court, 20 per soccer/baseball field Parks No requirement Any use not specified above 1 parking space per 30 m 2 of gross floor area.

All pedestrian access to the main door(s) of the building from the parking lot shall be designed with minimum 1.2 m (4 ft) width pedestrian pathway that are hard surfaced with asphalt, concrete, or unit pavers;

A structure of not more than 3.66 m (12 ft) in height and 4.65 m 2 (50.1 ft 2 ) in area may be erected on a parking lot for the use of attendants, and it shall be painted and maintained in good condition;
That have a minimum width of 2.5 m for the full length of the stall and contain at least one deciduous tree and a combination of shrubs, ornamental grasses and/or ground covers;
Provide groundfloor retail uses for no less than 50% of any street frontage to a minimum depth of no less than 6 m;
Parking garage entries on frontages shall not exceed 7 m wide and more than 30% of the buildings frontage.

other_requirements.requirement_text_raw: Parking spaces for trailer trucks, buses, delivery trucks, motor homes or other large vehicles shall have dimensions no less than 3.05 m (10 ft) by 12.2 m (40 ft) or larger, if necessary to accommodate longer vehicles.

A land use buffer, satisfactory to the Development Officer, consisting of a 1 m wide landscape treatment, a fence and/or mature trees (spaced no more than 6.1 m apart), or both, shall be provided where a parking lot abuts a residential zone or a building occupied for residential use in a commercial zone;
At a rate of one island per 15 parking spaces over the initial 30 stalls and one island per 30 stalls for the remaining parking spaces;
That may include a 1 m wide concrete walkway if the island width is increased to 3 m.
Off-street accessible parking spaces shall measure at least 3.6 m wide and shall contain:

Where the limits of the parking lot are defined by a curb, the parking lot shall be provided with a ramped curb as close as possible to the location to an accessible entrance or elevators and in no case, shall it be further than 50 m (164 ft) from the location which it is intended to serve, with the exception of a university or college which has a central campus and perimeter parking already established.
At least 0.45 m apart for a vertical rack or two-tier rack with a lift assist, or
At least 0.9 m apart for other types of rack. 8.13 BICYCLE PARKING (CLASS A)
Parking stalls shall be no less than 0.6 m (2 ft) long and 1.2 m (3.9 ft) high, and have an associated aisle of 1.5 m in width. 8.14 BICYCLE PARKING (CLASS B)
Be located outside of a building in a location that is visible and accessible from the street, within 20 m of a main door;

Include galvanized, powder coated or stainless steel racks spaced 0.90 m width apart with a clear bike parking length of 1.8 m. Table 8.5 Required Bicycle Parking Spaces by Use Use Bike Parking Requirement Ratio of Class A and B Multi-unit Dwelling 1 space per 4 units 80% class A, 20% class B Hotels 1 space/ 30 rooms 50% class A, 50% class B Commercial/Office 1 space/ 500 m 2 GFA 50% class A, 50% class B Entertainment Establishment 1 space/ 25 seats 20% class A, 80% class B Institutional uses 1 space/ 250 m 2 GFA 100% class B Parking Structure 1 space/ 20 parking stalls 100% class B Any other uses 1 space/ 500 m 2 GFA 50% class A, 50% class B /uni0020/uni0031/uni002E/uni0035/uni0020/uni006D/uni0020 /uni0020/uni0031/uni002E/uni0032/uni0020/uni006D/uni0020 /uni0020/uni0030/uni002E/uni0039/uni0020/uni006D/uni0020 /uni0042/uni0069/uni006B/uni0065/uni0020/uni0041/uni0069/uni0073/uni006C/uni0065 /uni0042/uni0069/uni006B/uni0065/uni0020/uni0052/uni0061/uni0063/uni006B Figure 8.3 Class B Bike Parking

review_flags.description: PDF text order was extracted with pypdf; verify section order, column flow, and table layout before normalization.
Some extracted text was not safely assigned to a labeled section and is preserved in content_blocks.
The source section contains table, figure, or schedule text. Values are preserved as extracted text and require source PDF layout review before normalization.
